title: Today's AI Brief: 11 Demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 in Action
summary: A look at Google's latest AI demos and the current state of AI hype
imageAlt: Image from source article for 11 demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 in action
imageCaption: 11 demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 in action
imageSourceTitle: 11 demos of Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 in action

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and welcome to today's AI brief. I've been digging into some fresh news signals, and I'm excited to share my thoughts with you. Google recently showcased 11 demos of their Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 models in action. These demos are worth checking out, but let's break them down and see what's interesting.

First up, Google's Gemini Omni and Gemini 3.5 demos are making waves. According to their blog post, Gemini Omni combines reasoning and creation, while Gemini 3.5 is designed to assist with various tasks. I'm looking at the demos, and what stands out to me is the potential for these models to change how we interact with technology. From what I can see, these demos show a glimpse of a future where AI is more integrated into our daily lives.

Also making headlines is the AI Hype Index, which suggests that AI might be losing its luster. A recent article from MIT Technology Review notes that AI got booed at a University of Arizona graduation ceremony. Apparently, former Google CEO Eric Schmidt was met with applause, but his AI-related comments were not. My read is that this might indicate a shift in how people perceive AI – it's no longer the shiny new thing it once was.

My quick read: the artificial intelligence story keeps getting bigger, but also stranger in very human ways. One moment it is strategy and infrastructure; the next, the Vatican is in the room. That is a pretty good sign this technology has left the lab and wandered into the town square.
